customer_addr:
2976 E. State St Suite 120 #211
Eagle, ID 83616

citations.narrative:
During public exhibition, many parakeets come dangerously close to people’s feet as they look for food on the floor and
under the benches where people are seated. The aviary is designed to allow the public to come in and have an interactive
experience with the parakeets, feeding them from hand-held plates that contain seed provided by the facility. There are
about 130 parakeets in the enclosure. People are seated on benches as they feed the birds. Even though only a small
amount of seed falls on the floor during feeding, it is enough to cause large numbers of birds to forage around people's
feet and the surrounding floor area. Guests are instructed to keep their feet and heals on the floor and shuffle as they
walk in and out of the enclosure. Despite these instructions, the inspector observed quests with heals raised in two
groups, and one parakeet passed under the raised heal of one of the guests. The presence of birds on the floor around
people's feet has resulted in the deaths of at least 4 birds at his facility in the last two years from being stepped on or
things being dropped on them. These deaths were cited on previous inspection reports. During public exhibition, care
must be taken so that there is minimal risk of harm to the birds with sufficient distance and/or barriers between the birds
and the general viewing public to assure the safety of animals.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.131(c)(1)  Repeat
   
Handling of animals. 
During public exhibition, many parakeets come dangerously close to people’s feet as they look for food on the floor and 
under the benches where people are seated. The aviary is designed to allow the public to come in and have an interactive 
experience with the parakeets, feeding them from hand-held plates that contain seed provided by the facility. There are 
about 130 parakeets in the enclosure. People are seated on benches as they feed the birds. Even though only a small 
amount of seed falls on the floor during feeding, it is enough to cause large numbers of birds to forage around people's 
feet and the surrounding floor area. Guests are instructed to keep their feet and heals on the floor and shuffle as they 
walk in and out of the enclosure. Despite these instructions, the inspector observed quests with heals raised in two 
groups, and one parakeet passed under the raised heal of one of the guests. The presence of birds on the floor around 
people's feet has resulted in the deaths of at least 4 birds at his facility in the last two years from being stepped on or 
things being dropped on them. These deaths were cited on previous inspection reports. During public exhibition, care 
must be taken so that there is minimal risk of harm to the birds with sufficient distance and/or barriers between the birds 
and the general viewing public to assure the safety of animals. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
